name: payments
context: Create and confirm payment intents, handle card payments, and manage payment methods
info: Payment intents are the core object for collecting payments in Stripe. A PaymentIntent tracks the lifecycle of a payment from creation through confirmation and capture. This skill covers creating payment intents with amount and currency, attaching payment methods (cards, bank transfers, wallets), confirming payments server-side or client-side, handling 3D Secure authentication for SCA compliance, and managing error states like declines and insufficient funds. The stripe-node SDK provides a clean interface for all payment operations.
triggers: charge a credit card with stripe

sections.title: Create payment intent
sections.body: Call `stripe.paymentIntents.create()` with `amount` (in smallest currency unit, e.g., cents), `currency` (ISO 4217), and optionally `payment_method`, `customer`, and `metadata`. The response includes `id`, `status`, and `client_secret`. Use `client_secret` for client-side confirmation with Stripe.js. See [[stripe/checkout#create-session]] for a hosted alternative.

sections.title: Confirm payment
sections.body: Confirm server-side with `stripe.paymentIntents.confirm(id, { payment_method })`. Status transitions: `requires_payment_method` -> `requires_confirmation` -> `requires_action` (if 3DS) -> `succeeded`. Listen for `payment_intent.succeeded` via [[stripe/webhooks]] for reliable confirmation.

sections.title: Payment methods
sections.body: Attach methods with `stripe.paymentMethods.attach(pmId, { customer })`. List with `stripe.paymentMethods.list({ customer, type: 'card' })`. Set default via `stripe.customers.update(id, { invoice_settings: { default_payment_method } })`. Supports cards, bank accounts, wallets, SEPA, and local methods. For recurring use, attach to a customer and reuse for [[stripe/subscriptions]].

sections.title: 3D Secure authentication
sections.body: When `status === 'requires_action'`, the payment needs 3D Secure authentication (SCA). Client-side: call `stripe.handleCardAction(clientSecret)` to show the 3DS modal. Server-side: check `paymentIntent.next_action` for redirect URL. After authentication, the intent moves to `succeeded` or `requires_payment_method` (if auth fails). Handle both outcomes via [[stripe/webhooks]].

sections.title: Error handling
sections.body: Stripe errors include `StripeCardError` (decline, insufficient funds), `StripeInvalidRequestError` (bad params), `StripeAuthenticationError` (bad key), and `StripeRateLimitError`. Card errors have a `code` field: `card_declined`, `insufficient_funds`, `expired_card`, `incorrect_cvc`. Always wrap API calls in try/catch. Use idempotency keys for safe retries.

links.context: Stripe ecosystem overview, API keys, and test mode
Use hosted checkout as a higher-level alternative
Reuse payment methods for recurring billing
Payment event types for async confirmation

related.note: Checkout provides a hosted UI — faster to integrate than custom payment forms
Always use webhooks for reliable payment confirmation, not just API responses
For recurring payments, use subscriptions instead of individual payment intents

scripts.name: Create Payment Intent
scripts.description: Create and confirm a Stripe PaymentIntent with amount, currency, and payment method

STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.description: Stripe secret API key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...)

amount.description: Payment amount in smallest currency unit (e.g., cents)

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., usd, eur, gbp)

paymentMethod.description: Payment method ID (pm_xxx) or test token (pm_card_visa)

customer.description: Customer ID (cus_xxx) to associate the payment with

status.description: Payment status: requires_payment_method, requires_confirmation, requires_action, succeeded, canceled

clientSecret.description: Client secret for client-side confirmation with Stripe.js

depends.reason: Requires a Stripe account and API key

author: dojo-community
